column2: Build familiarity with the pull up and set a baseline for consistency.
column3:
A: Bent over row (dumbbell or barbell) 3 sets of 8 reps. Moderate challenge

B: 3 sets, 5 reps per set (3 x 5) of banded pull ups. Use sufficient band tension that the last rep is challenging, but you can get full range of motion.
column4:
A: 2 sets of 15 reps each of banded face pulls and pull downs

B: 3 sets of 3 reps: Top of pull up hold for 3 seconds then 2 second negative (Stand on a box for support but try to use your legs as little as possible as you hold your chin over the bar for the entire three seconds, then a slow negative. Stand back up and repeat three times)
column5:
A: 2 sets of 30s active hang (pull down with the scaps, feel the activation in the back of your shoulder.

B: Three sets of rings rows to near failure (1 rep in reserve). Between sets, hold a 30s plank, then rest another 30s to 1min.

column2: Track grams of fruits and vegetables each day to get an average daily intake for the week.
Add some notes about how things felt, how the weight/band resistance is progressing, etc. This will be good context for me when we check in after a few weeks.
Starting to ramp up pulling volume, locking in consistency with hitting three days per week.
column3:
A: Bent over row (dumbbell or barbell) 3 sets of 8 reps. Go up 5-10# from last week.

B: 3 x 6 banded pull ups. Same band tension as last week.
column4:
A: 2 sets of 15 reps each of banded face pulls and pull downs

B: 3 sets of 3 reps: Top of pull up hold for 3 seconds then 3 second negative.
column5:
A: 2 sets of 35s active hang (pull down with the scaps, feel the activation in the back of your shoulder.

B: Three sets of rings rows to near failure (1 rep in reserve). Between sets, hold a 35s plank, then rest another 30s to 1min.

column2: Try and add 100g of fruits and vegetables compared to last week. This is especially helpful in the morning for breakfast (i.e. eat an apple, a large banana, etc. before leaving for work).
Balance continuing to increase pulling volume with starting to touch more difficult, less rep pulls.
column3:
A: Bent over row (dumbbell or barbell) 4 sets of 5 reps. Same moderate challenge, but with higher weight.

B: 3 x 3 banded pull ups. Less band tension - make these difficult
column4:
A: 2 sets of 15 reps each of banded face pulls and pull downs

B: 3 sets of 3 reps: Top of pull up hold for 4 seconds then 3 second negative.
column5:
A: 1 sets of 45s active hang (pull down with the scaps, feel the activation in the back of your shoulder.

B: Four sets of rings rows to less than failure (2-3 reps in reserve). Between sets, hold a 30s plank, then rest another 30s to 1min.

column2: Balance continuing to increase pulling volume with starting to touch more difficult, less rep pulls.

B: 3 x 3 banded pull ups. Less band tension - make these difficult
